Milan await CAS judgement

Milan are expected to have their appeal against a Europa League ban ruled on by the Court of Arbitration for Sport today.

The Rossoneri were excluded from the competition for Financial Fair Play breaches, as well as doubts over former owner Yonghong Li.

The Diavolo have now been taken over by Elliott Management after the Chinese owner failed to pay his debts, and Gazzetta dello Sport there is a three-pronged strategy to have the verdict overturned.

Firstly it will be pointed out that Elliott and Paul Singer are well known within the financial world, and don’t represent the unknown quantity that Li did.

Secondly, Milan will argue that there are no imminent debts or refinancing deadlines after the hedge fund’s takeover.

Finally, the club will seek to argue that the likes of Inter, Manchester City and Paris Saint-Germain have been treated less harshly.

The hearing will begin at 9.30 local time, with UEFA expected to essentially argue the same motives which saw them exclude Milan in the first place.

They will also point out though, that while Elliott may have stabilised Milan it has only done so for a future sale.

The hearing will conclude at around 18.00, with a verdict expected shortly after that.

Albertini: Milan return a ‘dream’

Former Milan midfielder Demetrio Albertini admits returning to the club would be a ‘dream’.

Albertini is a Milan legend, having made over 400 appearances for the Rossoneri between 1988 and 2002, winning five Scudetti and three European Cups.

The 45-year-old is currently out of work in football after spells with FIGC and Parma, but he made it clear a role back at San Siro was in his sights.

“I’ve never been contacted by anyone,” he said at the 2018 edition of the Gran Gala del Calcio.

“I’ve been taking my own path for several years, but it would be a dream if it crossed with Milan’s.

“It’s as if Milan have been wrapped in a layer of fog, but clarity is always the best thing.

“I think the fans feel like they’re on like on a rollercoaster: they go from moments of enthusiasm to ones of doubt and fear: they don’t deserve this.

“Elliott? It’s difficult to comment on them because the negotiations have been private and I don’t want to be superficial.

“I don’t know what the real intentions of the American fund are.”

Il Giornale claims that PSG will soon make an offer of €38m for Milan captain Leonardo Bonucci. His agent met with PSG earlier this week.

It’s reported that Milan have successfully lobbied to see the accounts for Paris Saint-Germain, Inter and Manchester City at the Court of Arbitration for Sport.

The Rossoneri are appealing a European ban, imposed by UEFA for Financial Fair Play violations.

Other big clubs have been treated in far more lenient fashion, so the club’s lawyers petitioned to see City, Inter and PSG’s accounts.

They believe they set an important precedent, as if those balance sheets show similar losses and debts it would raise questions about why those clubs weren’t excluded.

According to La Repubblica, CAS has accepted the request and ordered UEFA to produce the documents.

However, the FFP Club Financial Control Body did not in its motivations that Milan’s losses were only part of the reason for barring them from the Europa League.

There were also severe doubts over owner Yonghong Li, who has since been ousted by U.S hedge fund Elliott Management.

While that should partly assuage UEFA, the fund will look to sell the club when it can, which still creates ownership uncertainty.

Nikola Kalinic has admitted he does not deserve his World Cup runners-up medal, having been sent home from the tournament after Croatia's opening game.

The Milan striker refused to come on as a substitute in Croatia's 2-0 win over Nigeria, citing fitness issues, but was then kicked out of the squad by head coach Zlatko Dalic.

Kalinic had also missed out on the pre-tournament warm-up game with Brazil and was forced to sit out training due to back spasms. Dalic decided to send him home following the Nigeria victory, claiming that he needed players who were "fit and ready to play."

曼聯1比1戰平墨西哥美洲，以一場和波開始了美國熱身巡迴賽之旅，對於比賽結果，紅魔主教練摩連奴並不看重，他甚至做好了這次美國行輸得一塌糊塗的準備。

　　在與美洲對陣之前，摩帥就給媒體和球迷提前打起了預防針，他分析稱，曼聯這次巡迴賽可能會遭遇好幾場慘敗。根據安排，除了美洲隊，曼聯之後還要打聖荷西地震隊、AC米蘭、利物浦和皇馬，摩帥認為，這些隊的情況都比眼下的曼聯好。

　　「美洲隊正處在季前準備期的後程，聖荷西則正在賽季中，AC米蘭所有的球員都來了，因為意大利沒踢世界盃。」摩帥說，「然後是利物浦，我想除了英格蘭球員，軒達臣和阿諾特，他們的其他人都來了。」

　　「皇馬這邊，西班牙很早就從世界盃出局了，因此所有人也都來美國了。巴爾和賓施馬就沒去世界盃，因此我們面對的對手，能量都比我們足。所以我不會擔心是否會輸波，即使我們輸一些大比數，我也不會煩惱。」

　　談到賽季前的準備，摩連奴說：「一切都非常糟糕，然後我們就要打英超，轉播商對我們不友善，安排了週五的比賽，這讓事情更複雜了，因此我感到擔憂。」

　　「來這裡打利物浦、皇馬或者AC米蘭，我不會煩惱，即使大比數輸波也不會。我擔心的是，在沒有訓練準備的情況下就要去打英超，很多球員都不在隊里。但情況就是這樣，我們不得不努力，儘量用好現有的球員。」

　　「積極的一點是，年輕球員會有很好的機會，和我們一起訓練，並力爭成為曼聯一隊的一員。」
執雞
卡比仔
卡拉

其實唔係晦氣說話

上季開季懞帥帶得差，大家覺得可能係因為隊中新加盟球員較多，默契上仲係差啲，而且戰術上出咗好多問題
換咗加度素，整體表現都有所提升，聯賽歐霸意盃更加多場不敗，但係大家都睇得出，有啲賽事其實係贏運氣同埋士氣，而唔係靠表現
輸咗俾阿仙奴之後，球隊好似迷失咗方向，連場失分，教練部署亦都好似經常俾對手睇穿

依家轉會期，意甲其他前列球會都大肆增兵，實力上都有明顯提升，而米蘭暫時既收購暫時唔似會幫球隊增強實力，如果喺轉會期完之前，都買唔到啲比較強既球員，球隊可以靠既，就只有教練既功力，但係加度素暫時唔似可以為球隊帶黎新衝擊

所以下季真係唔樂觀

上季開季懞帥帶得差，大家覺得可能係因為隊中新加盟球員較多，默契上仲係差啲，而且戰術上出咗好多問題
換咗加度素，整體表現都有所提升，聯賽歐霸意盃更加多場不敗，但係大家都睇得出，有啲賽事其實係贏運氣同埋士氣，而唔係靠表現
輸咗俾阿仙奴之後，球隊好似迷失咗方向，連場失分，教練部署亦都好似經常俾對手睇穿

依家轉會期，意甲其他前列球會都大肆增兵，實力上都有明顯提升，而米蘭暫時既收購暫時唔似會幫球隊增強實力，如果喺轉會期完之前，都買唔到啲比較強既球員，球隊可以靠既，就只有教練既功力，但係加度素暫時唔似可以為球隊帶黎新衝擊

所以下季真係唔樂觀

同意ching 大部份
應該都係頭7架喇

呢家既米蘭無決心無戰術無士氣無錢

睇到撚撚真係無哂計去提升套戰術
即無隊波比唔到信心人，氣勢都無

戰術一早比人睇穿哂，suso 斬，岩岩個場睇左幾分鐘，又係比suso, 45度斬，無變過
